zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerard II van Loon於大約1147年出生在比利時利包格波格洛時，他的父親Lodewijk I van Loon39歲，母親Agnes de Metz34歲。他和Adélaïde von Geldern至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1194年9月2日在以色列מחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹ享年48歲，埋葬在比利時利包格庫林根。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerard II van Loon于大约1147年出生在比利时利包格波格洛时，他的父亲Lodewijk I van Loon39岁，母亲Agnes de Metz34岁。他和Adélaïde von Geldern至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1194年9月2日在以色列מחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹ享年48岁，埋葬在比利时利包格庫林根。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Loon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lon .
possibly a variant of English Lund , which is either a topographic name from Old Norse lundr ‘grove’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word.

variants.name: Lon
meanings: English: probably a variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lown .
Cambodian: written លន់, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘(to be) superb’. Compare   Lun   4.

Burmese (Shan): from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, of unexplained etymology. Compare   Lone   4. — Note: Since Shans do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Lowndes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lownes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lundy
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places called Lundie, for example one near Doune in Perthshire.
Irish: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French de la Launde ‘of the glade’. Compare Lown 1.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Lunde  .

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerard II van Loon was born about 1147, in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium, his father, Lodewijk I van Loon, was 39 and his mother, Agnes de Metz, was 34. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Adélaïde von Geldern. He died on 2 September 1194, in Acre, Northern, Israel, at the age of 48, and was buried in Kuringen, Limburg, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerard II van Loon est né environ 1147, à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique, son père, Lodewijk I van Loon, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Metz, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Adélaïde von Geldern. Il est décédé le 2 septembre 1194, à Acre, Northern, Israël, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Kuringen, Limbourg, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gerard II van Loon um 1147 in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lodewijk I van Loon 39 und seine Mutter Agnes de Metz 34. Er hatte mit Adélaïde von Geldern mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 2. September 1194 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Acre, Northern, Israel und wurde in Kuringen, Limburg, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard II van Loon è nato il circa 1147 nel Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Lodewijk I van Loon, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Metz, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Adélaïde von Geldern. Morì il 2 settembre 1194, nel Acre, Northern, Israele, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Kuringen, Limburgo, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerard II van Loonが約1147年にベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンで生まれた時，父Lodewijk I van Loonは39歳，母Agnes de Metzは34歳であった。彼はAdélaïde von Geldernとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1194年9月2日にイスラエルמחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹで48歳で死去し，ベルギーリンブルグクリンジェンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerard II van Loon(이)가 대략 1147년 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lodewijk I van Loon(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Metz(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Adélaïde von Geldern(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1194년 9월 2일 이스라엘 מחוז הצפון עַכּוֹ에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 림버그 커잉에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard II van Loon nasceu aproximadamente 1147, em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Lodewijk I van Loon, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Metz, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Adélaïde von Geldern. Ele faleceu em 2 de setembro de 1194, em Acre, Northern, Israel, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Kuringen, Limburgo, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerard II van Loon родился около 1147 г. в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Lodewijk I van Loon было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Metz, 34. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Adélaïde von Geldern. Он умер 2 сентября 1194 г. в местности עַכּוֹ, מחוז הצפון, Израиль в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности куринген, лимбург, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gerard II van Loon nació aproximadamente 1147, en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Lodewijk I van Loon, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Agnes de Metz, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Adélaïde von Geldern. Murió el 2 de septiembre de 1194, en Acre, Northern, Israel, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Kuringen, Limburgo, Bélgica.
